# UC-16.3.20 · Jira Automation Rule Loop or Comment Storm Detection

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Operational, Data Quality &middot; **Status:** Draft

*We look for a rule or a comment loop that is running away, so automation helps instead of filling the work item with noise.*

---

## Description

Runaway automation spams tickets and hides real human updates.

## Value

Catches feedback loops after bad JQL in Automation rules.

## Implementation

Throttle audit polling; correlate with rule deployment changes.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Install and configure: Splunk Add-on for Jira Cloud audit input.
- Data: Jira audit feed (`jira:cloud:audit:log`).
- For directory layout and inputs, see docs/implementation-guide.md.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Throttle audit polling; correlate with rule deployment changes.

### Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following search (then save as report or alert; tune time range and threshold):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="jira:cloud:audit:log" OR sourcetype="jira:audit"
| search action="commented" OR method="comment"
| stats count by issue_key, actor
| where count>30
| sort -count
```

Understanding this search
Runaway automation spams tickets and hides real human updates.

### Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the Jira issue page, board, or service project view for the same dates. Confirm issue keys, status, and assignment fields; reconcile timestamp fields you use in the report.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a dashboard, wire alert actions, and document ownership. Suggested view: Timeline (comments per minute), Table (issue × actor).

## SPL

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="jira:cloud:audit:log" OR sourcetype="jira:audit"
| search action="commented" OR method="comment"
| stats count by issue_key, actor
| where count>30
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Timeline (comments per minute), Table (issue × actor).

## Known False Positives

Bursts of comments and automation from integrations, copy-paste from other tools, or rule changes during an incident; tune the rule and exclude known bots with care.

## References

- [Splunk Add-on for Jira Cloud](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6211)
- [Source types — Splunk Add-on for Jira Cloud](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/AddOns/released/JiraCloud/Sourcetypes)
